items.situation: two friends deciding between a rock-climbing gym trip and a kayaking trip this weekend

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I can't decide what to do this weekend, the climbing gym downtown, or that kayaking trip Sam mentioned.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Kayaking sounds fun, but there's a thunderstorm forecast for both days.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh no, really? I hadn't checked that.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I saw it this morning. The climbing gym might be the safer bet anyway.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: True, and they've got beginner walls too, so we wouldn't need any real experience.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That settles it, then. I'll book us two beginner slots online before they fill up.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Perfect, text me once it's confirmed and I'll grab snacks for afterward.

questions.stem: What is the main topic of the conversation?

options.A: Choosing between a climbing gym and a kayaking trip for the weekend
options.B: Complaining about frequent storms in the area
options.C: Planning to buy new climbing equipment
options.D: Debating whether to cancel weekend plans entirely

questions.explanation: 两人主要讨论的是这个周末是去攀岩馆还是划皮划艇，最终因为天气原因选择了攀岩馆。B项对话中虽提到暴风雨预报，但只是次要原因，并非讨论主题；C项范围过窄，对话并未详细讨论购买装备；D项与两人计划继续出行的情况相反，他们并未取消周末计划。

questions.stem: What will the two friends most likely do this weekend?

options.B: Cancel their plans until next weekend
options.C: Book beginner climbing slots at the gym
options.D: Ask Sam to join them at the climbing gym

questions.explanation: 男生说"I'll book us two beginner slots online"，说明两人会去预订攀岩馆的初学者名额。A项是他们因暴风雨预报而放弃的选项；B项对话中没有依据，两人已经做出决定而非等待；D项对话中从未提到邀请Sam一起去，纯属无中生有。

items.situation: two students debating whether a newly opened board-game cafe is worth visiting for a study break

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Have you tried that new board-game cafe near the quad? I keep hearing mixed things about the cover charge.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I went last week. It's not bad, the charge covers unlimited games and free refills, so it evens out.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Oh, that's good to know. I assumed it'd be more like an arcade, paying per game.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Not at all. Popular games get reserved fast, so you'll want to arrive right at opening.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Got it. Should we go together after our exam Thursday, before the evening rush?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Works for me. I'll grab a table around five and text you the room number.

questions.stem: What is the main topic of the conversation?

options.A: Comparing dessert menus at two campus cafes
options.B: Deciding whether the new board-game cafe is worth visiting
options.C: Finding out when the board-game cafe opens
options.D: Agreeing the cafe is not worth the cost

questions.explanation: 两人主要讨论的是这家新开的桌游咖啡馆是否值得去，男生的亲身经验打消了女生对收费的顾虑，最终两人决定一起去。A项对话未提及甜点菜单；C项只是提到咖啡馆开门时间这一细节，并非讨论重点；D项与两人最终决定一起去的结论相反。

questions.stem: What will the two students most likely do on Thursday?

options.A: Arrive after the evening rush to avoid crowds
options.B: Order dessert before playing any games
options.C: Skip the cafe and go somewhere quieter instead
options.D: Get to the cafe early enough to reserve a popular game

questions.explanation: 男生提到热门桌游很快就被预订完，建议一开门就到，而女生也提议周四考试后趁晚高峰前一起去，因此可以推断两人会尽早到店以确保能玩到热门游戏。A项与他们计划避开晚高峰、提早前往的安排相反；B项对话中完全没有提到点甜点；C项与两人已经决定一起去的计划相矛盾，属于过度推断。

items.situation: two students sorting out a mixed-up food delivery order sent to the wrong dorm room

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Hey, did you order Thai food tonight? A delivery driver just dropped a bag at my door with your name on the receipt.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That's mine! I ordered from that new place on Fifth Street and I've been waiting almost forty minutes.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: The driver must've mixed up our room numbers. I'm just down the hall in 214, not 212.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: That makes sense. I'll come grab it right now before the noodles get cold.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: You should probably call the restaurant and complain about the mix-up.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Nah, it's not worth the hassle. I'll just add my room number to the delivery app note instead.

questions.stem: Why did the delivery end up at the wrong door?

options.A: The woman ordered the wrong dish
options.B: The delivery app charged the wrong room automatically
options.C: The driver mixed up the two students' room numbers
options.D: The man gave the wrong address when ordering

questions.explanation: 女生说"送餐员一定是把我们的房间号搞混了，我就在214，不是212"，说明送错门是因为送餐员搞混了房间号。A项对话中并未提及女生点错菜；B项对话没有说是配送软件自动扣错房间的费用；D项对话中没有说是男生自己填错了地址，而是送餐员搞混的。

questions.stem: What will the man most likely do to prevent this in the future?

options.A: Add his room number to the delivery app notes
options.B: Call the restaurant to file a complaint
options.C: Order from a different restaurant next time
options.D: Ask the front desk to hold his deliveries

questions.explanation: 男生说"不值得那么麻烦，我以后就在配送软件备注里加上房间号"，说明他打算在备注中注明房间号以避免再次出错。B项是女生建议但被男生以"不值得麻烦"为由拒绝的选项；C项对话中没有提到要换餐厅；D项对话中完全没有提及前台代收包裹这件事，属于过度推断。

items.situation: two roommates deciding whether to get a mini-fridge or a shared microwave for their room

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Our room budget for shared stuff came through. Should we get a mini-fridge or a microwave first?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Honestly, I use the dining hall microwave enough already. A fridge would save more trips downstairs.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: True, especially for leftovers and cold drinks. But fridges pull a lot of power, will that trip the outlet?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: I already checked the room's limit, it's fine unless we run the space heater too.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Good, that settles it. I'll order the fridge tonight and we can revisit the microwave next semester.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Sounds good. Let's just measure first, so it actually fits under the loft bed.

questions.stem: What did the man already check before agreeing to the mini-fridge?

options.A: Whether the dining hall microwave was available
options.B: The store's return policy for fridges
options.C: Whether the woman preferred a fridge over a microwave

questions.explanation: 男生说"我已经查过房间的电力上限了，没问题"，说明他事先确认的是房间的电力承载限度。A项对话中没有提到检查食堂微波炉是否可用；B项对话完全没有提到退货政策；C项对话中并没有说男生特意确认女生的偏好，两人是共同讨论决定的。

questions.stem: What will the two roommates most likely do before the fridge arrives?

options.A: Return the fridge if it doesn't fit under the bed
options.B: Measure the space under the loft bed
options.C: Buy a microwave as a backup instead
options.D: Ask maintenance to install an additional outlet

questions.explanation: 男生最后说"我们得先量一下尺寸，确保它能放到阁楼床下面"，说明两人在冰箱送到前会先测量空间。A项对话没有依据说明他们会因为尺寸问题退货，属于过度推断；C项对话中没有提到买微波炉作为备用，反而决定下学期再讨论微波炉；D项对话中完全没有提及联系维修部门加装插座。

items.situation: two students recommending study-focus apps to each other before exam week

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: Exam week is coming up and I can't focus at all. Do you use any study apps?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Yeah, I use an app called Forest, it grows a tree while you study, and the tree dies if you check your phone.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: That's kind of genius. Does it stop you from getting distracted, or is it just a reminder?

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: It's mostly a reminder, but not wanting to kill a virtual tree works better than I expected.

conversation.speaker: Woman
conversation.text: I'll definitely download it tonight. Maybe we can compare our study streaks after the first week.

conversation.speaker: Man
conversation.text: Deal. Let's check in Friday and see whose tree survived longest.

questions.stem: According to the man, what happens if he checks his phone while using the Forest app?

options.A: The virtual tree he is growing dies
options.B: The app sends him a warning notification
options.C: The app locks his phone automatically
options.D: His study streak resets to zero

questions.explanation: 男生说"如果你看手机，树就会死"，说明查看手机的后果是虚拟树死亡。B项对话中没有提到应用会发送警告通知；C项对话没有说应用会自动锁住手机；D项提到的"学习连胜"是他们打算之后互相比较的内容，并非查看手机的直接后果。

questions.stem: What will the two students most likely do on Friday?

options.A: Give up on the app if it doesn't help by then
options.B: Meet in person to quiz each other on course material
options.C: Compare how long their virtual trees survived
options.D: Ask their professor to extend the exam date

questions.explanation: 女生提议"周五对比一下我们的学习连胜"，男生回应"看看谁的树活得更久"，说明周五他们会比较各自虚拟树存活的时间。A项对话中没有依据说明他们会放弃这个应用，属于过度推断；B项对话中没有提到要面对面一起学习；D项对话中完全没有提到向教授申请延长考试时间，明显超出对话内容范围。
